title: Chronic Urticaria
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Chronic Urticaria
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Chronic Urticaria
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Chronic Urticaria** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Chronic Urticaria. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Chronic Urticaria, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Chronic Urticaria.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Chronic Urticaria
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

references.title: International EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI Guideline for Chronic Urticaria

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Chronic Urticaria?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Chronic Urticaria, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Chronic Urticaria?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Chronic Urticaria, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Chronic Urticaria requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Chronic Urticaria requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.
